arts.ttl: Newcastle ‘would be open’ to selling one of their highest-paid players
arts.sum: Newcastle United defender Kieran Trippier has been linked to the move away from the club in recent months. According to former Premier League scout Mick Brown, Newcastle have now made a definitive decision on the 34-year-old’s future and they are ready to entertain offers for him. The England international is no longer a regular starter […] The post Newcastle ‘would be open’ to selling one of their highest-paid players appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United face new PSR issue with January decision due on international pair
arts.sum: Newcastle United boss Eddie Howe has admitted that contracts no longer guarantee him complete control over holding on to his star names. And while question marks still remain above the futures of North Shields lad Sean Longstaff, Fabian Schar and Callum Wilson, Howe said that Financial Fair Play and Profit & Sustainability Rules are now arguably the deciding factor for some of his players, whether he wants to hold on to them or not.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United announce double 'best in class' PSR move inspired by Borussia Dortmund
arts.sum: Announcing the new Metrocentre store, Newcastle United ’s chief commercial officer Peter Silverstone said: "Our ambition is to bring Newcastle United supporters even closer to the club through a best-in-class retail experience. Newcastle United commercial supremo Peter Silverstone has pledged a "best in class" club shop experience ahead of the two key dates for the calendar in 2024.

arts.ttl: Newcastle preparing mammoth £80m bid for target that would rather move to Liverpool
arts.sum: Newcastle haven’t given up hope of landing Crystal Palace centre-back, Marc Guehi, though it’s believed the defender would prefer a move to Liverpool. The player has already admitted that the speculation surrounding his future last summer was tough to deal with. arts.ttl: Man United transfer news: Red Devils ahead of Newcastle United in race for former Manchester City star
arts.sum: Manchester United are reportedly ready to leapfrog Newcastle United and sign Bayern Munich forward LEroy Sane should an opportunity present itself.

arts.ttl: Alexander Isak contract warning to Newcastle as Arsenal and Chelsea eye swoop
arts.sum: Isak is said to be currently earning £120,000 per week, and there have been suggestions that Newcastle could hand Gordon and the Sweden international a pay increase that seems them earn a weekly wage of £200,000 - more than Bruno Guimareas, the best-paid player currently. A large number of the comments centred around whether or not Isak and Gordon deserve to be earning £200,000 per week at Newcastle.
